title: Ding Dong Bell
content:
Ding dong bell!
    Pussy's in the well!
    Who put her in?
    Little Tommy Lin.
    Who pulled her out?
    Little Tommy Stout.
    What a naughty boy was that
    To drown poor pussy-cat,
    Who ne'er did any harm,
    But killed all the mice in father's barn.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter Crane, titled "Ding Dong Bell", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
